name: Texas SNAP Food Benefits
description: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program providing food benefits on a Lone Star Card to eligible low-income Texans to purchase groceries.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families who meet federal income and resource guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 130% of federal poverty level for gross income; 100% for net income

name: Your Texas Benefits
description: Online portal to apply for and manage SNAP, Medicaid, TANF, and other public assistance programs in one secure place.
eligibilitySummary: Texas residents applying for or managing any HHS benefit program
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Texas Medicaid
description: State Medicaid program providing health coverage for low-income children, families, pregnant women, seniors, and people with disabilities.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant women, parents/caretakers, elderly, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by category; children generally up to 198% FPL; pregnant women up to 198% FPL

name: Texas Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP)
description: Low-cost health coverage for children in families who earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 in families with income too high for Medicaid
incomeLimitNotes: Family income up to 201% of federal poverty level

name: Healthy Texas Women
description: State program providing women's health and family planning services at no cost, including birth control, screenings, and postpartum care.
eligibilitySummary: Women ages 18-44 who are Texas residents, U.S. citizens or qualified immigrants, uninsured, and not currently pregnant
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Texas WIC Program
description: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children providing nutrition education, nutritious foods, breastfeeding support, and referrals.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women and children under 5 who meet income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 185% of federal poverty level

name: Texas Unemployment Benefits (TWC)
description: Unemployment insurance providing temporary income to workers who lost jobs through no fault of their own while they search for new employment.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own and meet wage and work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period; benefits based on past earnings

name: Texas Workforce Solutions - Job Search & Career Services
description: Network of workforce development offices providing free job search assistance, career training, resume help, and employment services as part of the American Job Center network.
eligibilitySummary: All Texas job seekers and employers; priority for veterans, public assistance recipients, and low-income individuals

name: Workforce Solutions Office Locator
description: Find your nearest Workforce Solutions office for in-person job search help, training programs, and career counseling services.
eligibilitySummary: Open to all Texas residents seeking employment assistance

name: SNAP Employment & Training Program
description: Program helping SNAP recipients gain skills, training, and work experience to find employment and achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: SNAP recipients who are required to participate in work activities or voluntarily seek employment assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Must be a current SNAP recipient

name: TANF Cash Assistance
description: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families providing cash payments to help families with children pay for food, clothing, housing, and other essentials.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children age 18 or younger who have very low or no income
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; a family of 3 generally must earn less than approximately $188/month

name: Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs (TDHCA)
description: State housing finance agency administering housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, and community development for low-income Texans.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals, families, and communities across Texas
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 80% of Area Median Family Income

name: TDHCA Tenant-Based Rental Assistance (TBRA)
description: Rental subsidies for up to 24 months for low-income households participating in a self-sufficiency program.
eligibilitySummary: Households with income at or below 80% of Area Median Family Income seeking rental assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Household income must not exceed 80% of Area Median Family Income (AMFI)

name: Help for Texans (TDHCA Resource Finder)
description: Online search tool to find local service providers for housing, utility, and community assistance programs funded by TDHCA.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texas resident seeking housing or community assistance resources

name: Comprehensive Energy Assistance Program (CEAP)
description: Utility assistance program helping low-income households meet immediate energy needs and providing energy education to control costs long-term.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households needing help with energy bills
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 150% of federal poverty level or 60% of state median income

name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
description: Program providing home weatherization improvements to reduce energy costs for low-income households through insulation, air sealing, and efficiency upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters; priority given to elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of federal poverty level

name: Texas RioGrande Legal Aid (TRLA)
description: Largest legal aid provider in Texas offering free legal counsel, representation, and education to low-income people in 68 Texas counties.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in TRLA's 68-county service area in Southwest Texas
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 125% of federal poverty level

name: Lone Star Legal Aid
description: Free civil legal services for low-income residents in 72 counties in eastern and Gulf Coast Texas, covering family law, housing, benefits, and more.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals at or below 125% of federal poverty guidelines in 72 eastern and Gulf Coast Texas counties
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 125% of federal poverty guidelines

name: Texas Legal Services Center (TLSC)
description: Statewide nonprofit providing free legal advice, advocacy, representation, and education to underserved Texans on civil legal issues.
eligibilitySummary: Income-eligible Texans needing civil legal assistance statewide
incomeLimitNotes: Income-eligible; varies by program

name: TexasLawHelp.org
description: Free online legal resource providing easy-to-read articles, guides, forms, and a legal aid directory for civil legal issues like family law, housing, and benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texas resident seeking legal information or referrals

name: 2-1-1 Texas
description: Free, anonymous 24/7 social service helpline connecting Texans with local resources for food, housing, child care, crisis counseling, and other essential services.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texas resident needing help finding social services or community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year
languages: English, Spanish, and 150+ languages via Language Line

name: Feeding Texas
description: Statewide network of 20 food banks serving all 254 Texas counties, providing food distribution and nutrition programs to over 4 million Texans annually.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texan facing food insecurity; find your local food bank by ZIP code

name: Houston Food Bank
description: Nation's largest food bank by distribution, providing access to over 200 million nutritious meals across 18 counties in southeast Texas.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in the 18-county southeast Texas service area

name: North Texas Food Bank
description: Food bank distributing food to over 400 food pantries and community organizations across 13 North Texas counties.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in the 13-county North Texas service area

name: San Antonio Food Bank
description: Food bank serving one of the largest and most diverse service areas in Southwest Texas, providing food and nutrition programs.
address: 5200 Enrique M. Barrera Pkwy, San Antonio, TX 78227
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing food insecurity in the Southwest Texas service area

name: Central Texas Food Bank
description: Largest hunger-relief charity in Central Texas providing food distribution and nutrition programs to families in need.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in the 21-county Central Texas service area

name: Tarrant Area Food Bank
description: Food bank delivering food to local pantries and programs across 13 counties in the Tarrant County region.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families facing hunger in the 13-county Tarrant area service region

name: Texas Veterans Commission
description: State agency providing claims assistance, employment services, education benefits, mental health support, and grants to Texas veterans and their families.
address: 1700 N. Congress Ave, Suite 800, Austin, TX 78701
eligibilitySummary: Texas veterans, their dependents, and survivors

name: TWC Veterans Employment Services
description: Employment services specifically for Texas veterans, matching veteran job seekers with employment opportunities through Workforce Solutions offices.
eligibilitySummary: Texas veterans seeking employment; priority of service for veterans in all TWC programs

name: TWC Child Care Services (Subsidized Child Care)
description: Financial assistance program providing child care scholarships to eligible families so parents can work, search for work, or attend school or job training.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children under 13 who need child care to work, search for employment, or attend education/training
incomeLimitNotes: Family income generally at or below 85% of state median income; varies by local workforce board

name: TWC Child Care Information for Parents
description: Resources helping Texas parents find quality child care, understand financial assistance options, and access child care scholarships.
eligibilitySummary: Texas parents seeking child care options and financial assistance

name: Houston 311
description: City of Houston's non-emergency service line for reporting problems, requesting city services, and accessing information about city programs.
eligibilitySummary: Any Houston resident or visitor needing non-emergency city services or information
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Harris County 311
description: Harris County's phone and web-based resource for government information and non-emergency services in unincorporated areas and areas outside Houston city limits.
eligibilitySummary: Harris County residents in unincorporated areas and outside Houston city limits

name: Harris County Community Services - Rental Assistance
description: Emergency rental and mortgage assistance for Harris County residents experiencing financial hardship, funded through local, state, and federal sources.
eligibilitySummary: Harris County residents below 80% area median income with proof of hardship
incomeLimitNotes: Must be below 80% average median income for Harris County

name: City of Houston Homebuyer Assistance Program (HAP)
description: Down payment and closing cost assistance of up to $50,000 for income-qualified first-time homebuyers within Houston city limits.
eligibilitySummary: First-time homebuyers within Houston city limits who meet income qualifications
incomeLimitNotes: Income-qualified; generally at or below 80% area median income

name: City of Houston Housing and Community Development Department
description: City department administering housing programs including homebuyer assistance, affordable housing development, and supportive services for Houston residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low- to moderate-income Houston residents seeking housing assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: DART Reduced Fare Program (Dallas)
description: Reduced transit fares for seniors 65+, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders on Dallas Area Rapid Transit buses and trains.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors age 65+, persons with disabilities, and Medicare card holders in the DART service area

name: Capital Metro Reduced Fare Program (Austin)
description: Reduced transit fares for seniors, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders on Austin's Capital Metro bus and rail services.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors, persons with disabilities, and Medicare cardholders in the Capital Metro service area

name: Texas Health and Human Services - Food Programs
description: Overview of all Texas food assistance programs including SNAP, WIC, school meals, and commodity distribution administered by HHS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Texas residents in need of food assistance
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program

name: Texas Food Bank Network Providers
description: HHS directory of all Feeding Texas food bank network providers across the state with contact information and service areas.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texan seeking food bank assistance

name: Texas Workforce Commission (TWC) - Main Portal
description: State agency overseeing workforce development, unemployment insurance, labor law, and child care services for all Texans.
eligibilitySummary: All Texas workers, job seekers, and employers

name: Texas DSHS HOPWA Program
description: Housing Opportunities for Persons with AIDS providing housing assistance and supportive services to low-income Texans living with HIV/AIDS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals living with HIV/AIDS and their families
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet HUD low-income guidelines

name: TexVet - Texas Veterans Resource Portal
description: Comprehensive online portal connecting Texas veterans to resources including benefits, healthcare, housing, employment, education, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: Texas veterans, service members, and their families

name: Texas Court Help
description: Official Texas courts website providing free self-help legal information, forms, and guides for navigating the Texas court system without an attorney.
eligibilitySummary: Any Texas resident needing help navigating the court system

name: Harris County Housing Authority
description: Housing authority administering Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers and public housing programs for low-income Harris County residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Harris County residents seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Must meet HUD income limits for Harris County
